name: Stripe Customers
description: Data contract for the Stripe Customers object (`/v1/customers` API). The Customer is the master record every paying SaaS account is anchored to — every subscription, invoice and charge in Stripe links back to a Customer ID. Sourced via the Stripe REST API and webhook stream (`customer.created`, `customer.updated`, `customer.deleted`).

domain.name: Finance
domain.description: Revenue, billing, and customer financial relationships.

informationProduct.name: SaaS Revenue Metrics
informationProduct.description: MRR, ARR, ARPU, churn, expansion — the SaaS revenue dashboard.

dataAsset.name: raw.stripe.customers
dataAsset.description: Daily snapshot of Stripe Customer objects with webhook-driven incremental updates layered on top. One row per customer per change event.

team.name: Revenue Operations
team.description: Business owner of the customer master record — reconciles Stripe customers against CRM accounts.

team.name: Data Engineering
team.description: Owns the Stripe API ingestion job and webhook stream consumer.

team.name: Finance Analytics
team.description: Primary consumer — builds MRR/ARR/cohort analyses on top of the customer master.

personas.name: Chief Revenue Officer (CRO)
personas.description: Sets revenue strategy; needs accurate customer counts and segmentation.

personas.name: Finance Analyst
personas.description: Builds the monthly revenue close — trusts this contract for customer counts.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Stripe-issued unique customer identifier. Always starts with `cus_` followed by a 14-character alphanumeric string. Stable for the life of the customer record.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Customer's email address. Primary contact for invoices and receipts. Treated as PII — restrict access to Finance + RevOps.

columns.name: name
columns.description: Customer's full legal name (B2C) or company name (B2B). Free-text — Stripe does not validate.

columns.name: description
columns.description: Internal description set by the merchant when creating the customer. Often used to store a CRM account ID for cross-reference.

columns.name: currency
columns.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in lowercase (e.g. `usd`, `gbp`, `nzd`). The currency the customer will be charged in. Set on first invoice and immutable afterwards.

columns.name: balance
columns.description: Customer's account balance in the smallest currency unit (cents for USD). Negative = credit on account; positive = amount owed. Used to apply credits against future invoices.

columns.name: delinquent
columns.description: Boolean — `true` if the customer has unpaid invoices past their due date. Drives dunning workflows and at-risk customer flags.

columns.name: tax_exempt
columns.description: Tax exemption status: `none`, `exempt`, or `reverse`. Drives whether tax is applied on invoices.

columns.name: created
columns.description: Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch, UTC) when the customer was first created in Stripe. The acquisition date for cohort analysis.

columns.name: _extracted_at
columns.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the row was extracted from Stripe. Set by the ingestion job. Used for reconciliation and to identify late-arriving updates.

glossaryTerms.name: Customer
glossaryTerms.description: An entity (person or business) that has at least one active or historical paid relationship with us. In SaaS revenue accounting, equivalent to a Stripe Customer with one or more Subscriptions.

glossaryTerms.name: Delinquent
glossaryTerms.description: A customer with one or more invoices past their due date. Triggers dunning workflows and excludes the customer from active MRR until resolved.

deliveryTypes.name: Snowflake
deliveryTypes.description: Loaded into the `raw.stripe` schema in Snowflake via Fivetran every 6 hours.

deliveryTypes.name: Stripe API
deliveryTypes.description: Source system — REST API at https://api.stripe.com/v1/customers plus webhook events.

trustRules.name: id non-null + Stripe format
trustRules.description: Every row must have a populated `id` matching the Stripe customer ID format (`cus_` prefix + 14 chars).
trustRules.rule: id IS NOT NULL AND id LIKE 'cus_%' AND LENGTH(id) = 18

trustRules.name: id is unique
trustRules.description: Customer ID is the natural key — each ID may appear at most once per snapshot.
trustRules.rule: COUNT(*) GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 = 0

trustRules.name: Email format valid
trustRules.description: When email is populated it must contain `@` and `.` — Stripe doesn't enforce this so we check on landing.
trustRules.rule: email IS NULL OR email REGEXP '^.+@.+\..+$'

trustRules.name: Currency is a valid ISO 4217 code
trustRules.description: Currency must be a known three-letter lowercase ISO 4217 code that matches our supported list.
trustRules.rule: currency IS NULL OR currency IN ('usd','gbp','eur','aud','nzd','cad','jpy','sgd')

trustRules.name: PII columns classified
trustRules.description: email and name are PII and must be marked `confidential`. Audited quarterly.
trustRules.rule: Columns email and name carry classification = 'confidential' on the contract definition

trustRules.name: Freshness within 12 hours
trustRules.description: Latest `_extracted_at` across the dataset must be within the last 12 hours — drift would mean Fivetran is broken.

dataSyncs.name: Refresh frequency
dataSyncs.description: Bulk snapshot via the Stripe API every 6 hours; webhook stream applies incrementals continuously.

dataSyncs.name: Webhook event latency
dataSyncs.description: p95 latency from Stripe webhook event to row appearing in Snowflake.

dataSyncs.name: Pipeline uptime
dataSyncs.description: Monthly availability target for the customer ingestion pipeline.

lineage.name: Stripe Billing Platform
lineage.description: The system of record for all paying customer relationships.

lineage.name: Fivetran Stripe Connector
lineage.description: Hourly pull from Stripe REST API into Snowflake `raw.stripe` schema.
